title: Wireless Client Experience Score by SSID
dataSources: `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:clienthealth` filtered to wireless clients, enriched by `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:client`
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" connectionType="WIRELESS" | stats avg(healthScore) as avg_experience avg(rssi) as avg_rssi dc(macAddress) as client_count by ssid | eval experience_rating=case(avg_experience>=8,"Excellent",avg_experience>=6,"Good",avg_experience>=4,"Fair",1==1,"Poor") | sort avg_experience
description: Calculates a per-SSID wireless client experience score combining health, signal strength, and client count to identify which wireless networks deliver the best and worst experience.
value: Different SSIDs serve different purposes and user populations. Per-SSID experience scoring reveals which wireless networks need optimization.
implementation: Use the **TA‚Äôs existing inputs** (no custom Intent poller for this UC if the fields are present): `clienthealth` from `/dna/intent/api/v1/client-health` and `client` from `/dna/intent/api/v1/client` (or the TA‚Äôs equivalent endpoints) into `cisco:dnac:clienthealth` and `cisco:dnac:client`. This Splunk search primarily uses `cisco:dnac:client` with `connectionType=WIRELESS` to segment wireless clients, then `stats` by `ssid` using `healthScore` and `rssi` (confirm field names in your data ‚Äî Cisco may use `clientHealth` or similar; add `| fieldsummary` in Search if needed). Enable both inputs in the Catalyst TA to `index=catalyst`.
visualization: Bar chart of `avg_experience` by `ssid`, table (ssid, experience_rating, client_count, avg_rssi), single value of worst SSID by score.
knownFalsePositives: Guest, IoT, or low-priority SSIDs can sit at low scores in Assurance while the business-critical SSID next to them is fine. A short dip during roaming-heavy events or after a code upgrade can also look worse than the user experience really is for that hour.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (7538) with `client` and, if available, `clienthealth` inputs to `index=catalyst` (Intent API client listing and health).

Step 1 ‚Äî TA data path (Catalyst Center)
‚Ä¢ `cisco:dnac:client` should include `connectionType` (use `WIRELESS`), `ssid`, `macAddress`, and a numeric experience field such as `healthScore` (confirm with `| fieldsummary`).
‚Ä¢ `cisco:dnac:clienthealth` is optional for a **join** on `macAddress` if you need richer per-client metrics than this aggregate gives.

Step 2 ‚Äî Search

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" connectionType="WIRELESS" | stats avg(healthScore) as avg_experience avg(rssi) as avg_rssi dc(macAddress) as client_count by ssid | eval experience_rating=case(avg_experience>=8,"Excellent",avg_experience>=6,"Good",avg_experience>=4,"Fair",1==1,"Poor") | sort avg_experience
```

Step 3 ‚Äî Optional join
`index=catalyst (sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" OR sourcetype="cisco:dnac:clienthealth")` with `join` or `stats` on `macAddress` when the TA stores scores only on the health sourcetype.

Step 4 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ Compare a few SSIDs to **Catalyst Center > Wireless > Clients** and any Assurance ‚Äúexperience‚Äù view for the same time window. Watch for `ssid` that is BSSID or internal ‚Äî normalize in **props** if required.

Step 5 ‚Äî Operationalize and troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **Weekly** Wi-Fi review: focus on the bottom SSIDs, not a single outlier client. **No `ssid`:** the TA may use `wlan` or a profile name ‚Äî add `coalesce` in `stats by` until fields match the UI. **RSSI in wrong units:** some builds store dBm as negative; keep comparisons consistent. **This is not a site survey** ‚Äî it is a fleet hint for triage, not a replacement for on-site validation.
grandmaExplanation: We rate the experience quality for each wireless network by name, showing which ones are giving users a good experience and which ones need work.
